title: Parse the snapshot surface the anchor join needs, and prove it on the live production path
action: The gateway polls herdr's session.snapshot on every refresh and throws away most of the envelope. Everything this feature does rests on stopping that. FIXTURE MUST BECOME TRACKED TEST DATA -- .bee/spikes/ is gitignored (.gitignore:10), so a test reading from there is a compile error in CI and in every other clone. Your FIRST step is to copy .bee/spikes/new-shell-new-agent/live-snapshot.json and .bee/spikes/new-shell-new-agent/expected-anchors.json into src/herdr/testdata/ and commit them with the cell; both paths are in this cell's files for that reason. Reference them from tests with include_str!, not a runtime filesystem read. ENVELOPE NESTING: live-snapshot.json is the INNER snapshot object -- its top-level keys are agents, panes, layouts, tabs, workspaces, focused_*_id, version, protocol, with NO outer snapshot wrapper. socket.rs:228-231 does result.get("snapshot"), so parse_snapshot takes the OUTER value and your test must wrap the fixture: parse_snapshot(&json!({ "snapshot": <fixture> })). Do not change parse_snapshot's contract to match the fixture -- that would desync it from the real snapshot() call path and destroy the point of the seam. The fixture content is: it is a REAL captured envelope from herdr 0.7.4 protocol 16 (5 workspaces, 5 tabs, 8 panes, 5 layouts, 7 agents; home paths and user@host neutralized, nothing else altered). Do not hand-write a fixture from the schema -- authoring both the fixture and the parser from the same reading of the schema proves only that you were self-consistent. WHAT TO ADD in src/herdr/wire.rs: the panes[] array, the layouts[] array, active_tab_id on the workspace type, cwd and foreground_cwd on the pane type, and the three top-level focused_workspace_id/focused_tab_id/focused_pane_id fields. Name the new types Pane and PaneLayout so cell 2's tests are not coupled to a guess. Model PaneLayout as workspace_id, tab_id, and focused_pane_id ONLY -- do NOT model splits[], area, or the layout's own inner panes[]: PaneLayoutSplit.ratio is a float and would break the existing #[derive(Eq)] on Snapshot (wire.rs:113), and removing that derive is out of scope. NAME COLLISION, read twice: PaneLayoutSnapshot carries its own panes[] of geometry objects, which is NOT the top-level panes[] of PaneInfo that carries cwd/foreground_cwd. Resolving a pane against the wrong one compiles, matches, and yields no cwd. ABSENCE POLICY: parse-tolerant at the serde layer -- #[serde(default)] on the new fields, which is needed anyway so the existing fixture at wire.rs:255 (no active_tab_id) keeps parsing -- but do NOT extend socket.rs's silent .ok() swallow to the new arrays. socket.rs currently holds two contradictory precedents in one function: a missing agents[] is a hard HerdrError::Malformed (socket.rs:232-235) while workspaces/tabs silently become empty (socket.rs:240-249). panes[] and layouts[] are required in herdr's schema (SessionSnapshot.required), so their absence means a broken or older server, not a normal case. THE PRODUCTION PATH IS THE POINT: socket.rs:228-254 does NOT deserialize Snapshot via serde -- it pulls fields off the Value by hand and ends in a Snapshot{..} struct literal at :250. The compiler will force you to touch that literal, but writing `panes: Vec::new(), layouts: Vec::new()` compiles, satisfies every fixture test, and ships an empty panes[] against a live herdr forever. That is verbatim the 2026-07-18 incident. socket.rs has zero tests over snapshot() because it does I/O inline, so extract a pure `fn parse_snapshot(result: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<Snapshot>` from socket.rs:226-255 and test it against the captured envelope. That seam extraction is real work and is in scope for this cell. All three files need substantive changes -- there is no 'turns out it did not need one' outcome here. Do not implement the anchor-resolution join; that is cell new-shell-new-agent-2. TEST NAMING IS PART OF THE CONTRACT: every test you add for this cell must be named with the prefix envelope_ (for example envelope_parses_panes_and_layouts, envelope_missing_new_fields_still_parses, envelope_socket_parse_populates_new_arrays, envelope_fake_seed_joins). The cell's verify counts passing envelope_ tests and requires at least 4; it deliberately fails on an untouched tree, so a green verify is evidence the work exists rather than evidence the suite was already green.

truths: Snapshot parses panes[], layouts[], and the three top-level focused_*_id fields
The workspace type carries active_tab_id; the pane type carries cwd and foreground_cwd

SocketHerdr's snapshot path populates panes[], layouts[], active_tab_id, cwd and foreground_cwd from a full response envelope, proven by a test over an extracted pure parse function -- not merely by compiling
A snapshot missing any of the new arrays or fields still parses without error at the serde layer
Every pre-existing wire.rs fixture parses to exactly the values it did before

For every workspace FakeHerdr seeds, the layouts[] entry for that workspace's active tab names a focused_pane_id that exists in the seeded panes[], and the seed includes at least one pane absent from agents[]
artifacts: A pure fn parse_snapshot(&serde_json::Value) -> Result<Snapshot> extracted from src/herdr/socket.rs, with a test feeding it the tracked fixture src/herdr/testdata/live-snapshot.json via include_str! and asserting panes[], layouts[], and active_tab_id all come through non-empty
Fixture tests in src/herdr/wire.rs covering panes[], layouts[], and active_tab_id
A test proving a snapshot with the new fields absent still parses
A test in src/herdr/fake.rs's existing mod tests (alongside snapshot_has_all_statuses, fake.rs:213) asserting the seeded panes[]/layouts[] join to each other and to the seeded workspaces/tabs
At least 4 tests named with the envelope_ prefix, all passing
src/herdr/testdata/live-snapshot.json and src/herdr/testdata/expected-anchors.json committed as tracked test data, copied verbatim from .bee/spikes/new-shell-new-agent/
key_links: docs/history/new-shell-new-agent/CONTEXT.md D10 names the exact fields the join needs
docs/history/learnings/critical-patterns.md:20 -- three population sites, and --demo IS FakeHerdr (src/main.rs:193)
src/herdr/testdata/live-snapshot.json -- the tracked wire capture the tests read; .bee/spikes/new-shell-new-agent/ holds the copy source only
prohibitions: Do not hand-write a snapshot fixture from schema.json when the captured live envelope exists
Do not model PaneLayout's splits[], area, or inner panes[] -- the float ratio breaks Snapshot's Eq derive
Do not remove or weaken the Eq/PartialEq derives on Snapshot to make room
Do not leave panes[] or layouts[] hardcoded empty in socket.rs's struct literal
Do not extend socket.rs's silent .ok() fallback to the new required arrays
Do not implement the anchor-resolution join -- that is cell 2
Do not add any herdr create verb (tab.create, agent.start) -- that is slice 2

Do not change any existing parsed value; note that adding the new fields to existing Snapshot{..} literals at wire.rs:272-276, wire.rs:290-294, socket.rs:250-254 and fake.rs:64 is expected and is not a weakening
Do not reuse agents[] as a substitute for panes[] -- the live capture has an anchor pane that is a plain shell absent from agents[]
Do not read a fixture from .bee/spikes/ in committed test code -- that directory is gitignored and absent in CI
Do not change parse_snapshot's contract so it accepts the inner snapshot object; wrap the fixture in the test instead

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- envelope_ 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. ([4-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) passed'

trace.outcome: Snapshot now parses panes[], layouts[], per-workspace active_tab_id, pane cwd/foreground_cwd and the top-level focused_*_id; extracted a pure parse_snapshot seam from socket.rs and proved the live extraction path against the tracked live-snapshot.json capture; FakeHerdr seeds a joinable panes/layouts set including a plain shell anchor absent from agents[]. 6 envelope_ tests pass.
